mechanism_narrative: Robust consumer spending, proxied by retail and food services sales, signals strong corporate earnings growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon. This is an always-on trend signal.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales recovered rapidly post-pandemic, suggesting sustained demand momentum as of early 2021.
regime_hint: economic recovery with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: If RSAFS declines for three consecutive months while the S&P 500 still posts positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of overlapping windows, or if the rank IC of RSAFS changes on forward returns is below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below the key 5% threshold signals labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days. The signal is triggered when UNRATE falls below 5%.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 5.1% as of February 2021, near the 5% threshold and trending downward.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE falls below 5%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative more often than positive (win rate <= 50%) or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation rises month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains unchanged at its zero lower bound, real interest rates decrease, incentivizing investment in equities. The interaction of rising CPI and a flat Fed funds rate produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.2% and FEDFUNDS unchanged (change <= 0.01%)
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.14% in February 2021 and CPIAUCSL was rising from low levels.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: If during the specified trigger condition the S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of cases, or the rank IC of the joint signal is below 0.05 over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-pandemic recovery phase, rising industrial production directly boosts corporate profits and economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns. The signal is always active but its predictive power is strongest when the economy is in an industrial expansion regime.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production in early 2021 was recovering from pandemic lows, with year-over-year growth turning positive.
failure_condition: If over any 3-year period the rank correlation between INDPRO monthly changes and subsequent 60-day returns is less than 0.02, or if the average return in the regime is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging above the 1500 thousand unit level signals strong construction activity and consumer confidence, which historically leads to positive short-term equity returns. The threshold trigger of HOUST > 1500 activates a bullish signal over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts in January 2021 were 1206 thousand, below 1500, but the trend was upward.
failure_condition: If after HOUST exceeds 1500, the 5-day forward return is negative more than 50% of the time or the average return is not statistically positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy labor market that supports aggregate demand and corporate earnings. Over a 60-trading-day horizon, rising payrolls consistently lead to positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll employment in February 2021 was 142.3 million, still well below pre-pandemic levels but recovering.
failure_condition: If over any 2-year period the Rank IC of month-over-month PAYEMS changes on 60-day returns is below 0.03, or if the forward returns are negative more often than positive during sustained employment gains.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is extremely low (below 0.25%) and housing starts increase month-over-month by more than 5%, the combination of cheap credit and strong housing demand amplifies economic optimism and equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and one-month change in HOUST > +5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.14% in February 2021; housing starts were recovering from pandemic lows.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with accelerating housing construction
failure_condition: If during the joint trigger condition the S&P 500 20-day return is negative more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not significantly greater than the unconditional average (z-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (growth above 1%) signals strong manufacturing momentum, which lifts cyclicals and boosts short-term equity returns. The threshold trigger is INDPRO monthly change > 1%, with a 5-trading-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production had positive month-over-month growth in early 2021 as the economy reopened.
failure_condition: If after the trigger the 5-day forward return is negative more often than positive (win rate <= 50%), or the average return is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).
